title: Spec Invalidate-Metadata
description: Sometimes metadata needs to be forcibly marked stale without deleting the file or losing fields like `dependsOn`. The `specd spec invalidate-metadata` command marks a spec's metadata as stale by removing its `contentHashes` field, forcing regeneration on the next pass.
context: Sometimes metadata needs to be forcibly marked stale without deleting the file or losing fields like `dependsOn`. The `specd spec invalidate-metadata` command marks a spec's metadata as stale by removing its `contentHashes` field, forcing regeneration on the next pass.

rules:
The command is registered as `invalidate-metadata <specPath>` on the `spec` parent command. It accepts:

- `--format <fmt>` — output format: `text|json|toon` (default `text`)
- `--config <path>` — path to `specd.yaml`

The `<specPath>` argument uses the same `workspace:capability-path` syntax as other `spec` subcommands.
On success, text format outputs: `invalidated metadata for <workspace:path>`

rules.requirement: Error — spec not found or no metadata
rules: If the spec does not exist, or has no metadata, the command writes `error: spec '<specPath>' not found or has no metadata` to stderr and exits with code 1.

constraints: The command contains no business logic — all work is delegated to the InvalidateSpecMetadata use case
The command never reads or writes the filesystem directly for spec content — it uses the kernel

scenarios.name: Accepts specPath as argument
when: specd spec invalidate-metadata auth/login is invoked
then: the command accepts <specPath> as the spec to invalidate

scenarios.name: Supports --config option
when: specd spec invalidate-metadata auth/login --config /tmp/specd.yaml is run
then: the command uses the specified config path

scenarios.name: Successful invalidation in text format
when: the invalidation succeeds and format is text
then: the command outputs invalidated metadata for default:auth/login

scenarios.name: Successful invalidation in JSON format
when: the invalidation succeeds and format is json

scenarios.requirement: Error — spec not found or no metadata
scenarios.name: Unknown spec exits 1
when: the spec does not exist in the workspace
then: the command writes error: spec '<specPath>' not found or has no metadata to stderr and exits with code 1

scenarios.requirement: Error — spec not found or no metadata
scenarios.name: No metadata exits 1
given: a spec that exists but has no metadata
when: specd spec invalidate-metadata auth/login is invoked
then: the command writes error: spec 'auth/login' not found or has no metadata to stderr and exits with code 1
